title: argyroxiphium sandwicense subsp. sandwicense
intro: Argyroxiphium sandwicense subsp. sandwicense, also known as the Hawaiian silversword or Ma'o hau hele, is a critically endangered succulent plant endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of saponins, which are also found in other plants with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Hawaiian ceremonies and rituals.
